api_pattern: OpenAI Responses-style cleanup request, simulated offline
clean_excerpt:
[Chunk 001: chunk-001.mp3.txt]
Welcome back to the applied learning seminar. Today we are connecting source
materials to a concept index and a coverage report.

The first step is to build a manifest of the source files so every transformed
artifact can be traced back to the original input. We should keep the video
files private and only promote reviewed summaries or synthetic examples.

Next, split the audio into predictable segments. If the transcription model is
uncertain, keep the [unclear] marker instead of inventing a term. Then clean
obvious spelling errors such as manifest and retrieval.

Finally, the analyst reviews the cleaned transcript excerpt before it becomes
part of a downstream retrieval or reporting workflow.

[Chunk 002: chunk-002.mp3.txt]
Welcome back to the applied learning seminar. Today we are connecting source
materials to a concept index and a coverage report.

The

output_contract.correction_notes: recorded separately from cleaned transcript text

system_prompt: Clean a machine transcript for downstream analysis. Correct obvious recognition errors, preserve the speaker's meaning and sequence, keep uncertainty markers such as [unclear], and return only cleaned transcript text plus structured correction notes.
